reasoning: The FRED data shows the TGA value as of Nov 5, 2025 was 942.7 billion. The latest Treasury presentation from Nov 5 assumes an end-of-year 2025 cash balance target of 850 billion, and an end-of-March 2026 target of 850 billion. Because the current level (942.7B) is considerably higher than the 850B target for both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, the TGA is expected to gradually decrease as the Treasury manages issuance and outlays towards that goal. Consequently, the probability of the balance increasing above 942.7B is relatively low in the short term. The FRED statistical tool gives a 35% combined historical/trend probability of exceeding the threshold for the near term. We reflect this drop toward the target with ~0.30 for the nearest date, gradually increasing our uncertainty to 0.40 for the 3-month horizon (Feb 2026) due to typical cash flow volatility and the longer timeframe.
